description: Embedding a password, API key, or token directly in a .tf file or variable default, which then also lands in plaintext state.
body: Failure mode: a credential is written as a literal in HCL or a committed tfvars, so it is exposed in version control AND copied into the plaintext state file. Detection signals: grep finds a key/password literal in .tf/tfvars; state pull reveals the secret in cleartext; a scanner flags the repo. Do not hardcode secrets in config. Safer fallback: inject secrets via environment variables or a secret-manager data source, mark variables/outputs sensitive, encrypt and restrict the state backend, and rotate any secret that was committed or stored in state.
